claim: Risedronate reduces risk of vertebral and non-vertebral fractures.

result.claim: Risedronate reduces risk of vertebral and non-vertebral fractures.
result.reasoning: The claim that Risedronate reduces the risk of vertebral and non-vertebral fractures is strongly supported by the evidence. A systematic review and meta-analysis (Paper ID: 24090054) explicitly states that risedronate demonstrated convincing evidence for both non-vertebral and vertebral fracture risk reductions. Furthermore, a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial (Paper ID: 19878070) provides specific data, showing that treatment with 5 mg/d of risedronate decreased the cumulative incidence of new vertebral fractures by 41% over 3 years and reduced the cumulative incidence of nonvertebral fractures by 39% over 3 years. These are high-quality studies providing consistent and clear evidence.

evidence.text: Treatment with 5 mg/d of risedronate decreased the cumulative incidence of new vertebral fractures by 41% over 3 years.
evidence.source: Effects of risedronate treatment on vertebral and nonvertebral fractures in women with postmenopausal osteoporosis: a randomized controlled trial. Vertebral Efficacy With Risedronate Therapy (VERT) Study Group.

evidence.text: Total, vertebral, nonvertebral, and hip fractures were significantly lower in refill-compliant and persistent patients.
evidence.source: Adherence to Bisphosphonate Therapy and Fracture Rates in Osteoporotic Women: Relationship to Vertebral and Nonvertebral Fractures From 2 US Claims Databases

evidence.text: Compliance rates of 75% and higher were associated with more pronounced fracture risk reduction.
evidence.source: Adherence to Bisphosphonate Therapy and Fracture Rates in Osteoporotic Women: Relationship to Vertebral and Nonvertebral Fractures From 2 US Claims Databases

evidence.text: Alendronate demonstrated evidence for both non-vertebral and vertebral fracture risk reductions.
evidence.source: Osteoporosis therapy: an example of putting evidence-based medicine into clinical practice

evidence.text: Risedronate demonstrated evidence for both non-vertebral and vertebral fracture risk reductions.
evidence.source: Osteoporosis therapy: an example of putting evidence-based medicine into clinical practice

evidence.text: The incidence of new vertebral fractures was detected by semiquantitative assessments of radiographs.
evidence.source: Effects of risedronate treatment on vertebral and nonvertebral fractures in women with postmenopausal osteoporosis: a randomized controlled trial. Vertebral Efficacy With Risedronate Therapy (VERT) Study Group.

evidence.text: Total, vertebral, nonvertebral, and hip fractures were significantly lower in refill-compliant and persistent patients.
evidence.source: Adherence to Bisphosphonate Therapy and Fracture Rates in Osteoporotic Women: Relationship to Vertebral and Nonvertebral Fractures From 2 US Claims Databases

evidence.text: The cumulative incidence of new vertebral fractures was 16.3% in the placebo arm.
evidence.source: Effects of risedronate treatment on vertebral and nonvertebral fractures in women with postmenopausal osteoporosis: a randomized controlled trial. Vertebral Efficacy With Risedronate Therapy (VERT) Study Group.

evidence.text: The cumulative incidence of nonvertebral fractures over 3 years was reduced by 39% in the 5 mg/d risedronate arm.
evidence.source: Effects of risedronate treatment on vertebral and nonvertebral fractures in women with postmenopausal osteoporosis: a randomized controlled trial. Vertebral Efficacy With Risedronate Therapy (VERT) Study Group.

evidence.text: The study measured the incidence of new vertebral fractures.
evidence.source: Effects of risedronate treatment on vertebral and nonvertebral fractures in women with postmenopausal osteoporosis: a randomized controlled trial. Vertebral Efficacy With Risedronate Therapy (VERT) Study Group.
